mechanism: CLE peptide ligand binds LRR-RLP receptor FEA2
CLV3-like peptide perceived by TD1 (CLV1 ortholog) LRR receptor kinase
ZmFCP1 also signals through FEA2 (shared receptor with ZmCLE7)
ZmFCP1 peptide ligand binds FEA3 LRR-RLP receptor in the rib zone (parallel arm)
FEA2 receptor couples to heterotrimeric G-alpha subunit CT2 in complex
FEA2 receptor partners with ZmCRN pseudokinase in the signaling complex
TD1 LRR-RLK relays CLV3-like CLE signal to restrict meristem proliferation
G-alpha CT2 relays CLV signal from FEA2 complex to restrict stem-cell proliferation
ZmCRN pseudokinase in FEA2 complex restricts meristem proliferation
FEA3-ZmFCP1 signaling directly represses ZmWUS1 expression in the rib zone (canonical CLE-WUS repression)
bZIP TF FEA4 promotes peripheral differentiation and limits meristem
MSCA1/ABPH2 glutaredoxin post-translationally inhibits FEA4 activity (dominant Abphyl2 allele derepresses meristem)
Five ZmEPFL peptide ligands bind and activate ZmER1 LRR-RLK (redundant composite ligand)
ZmER-EPFL signaling restricts ZmWUS1 activity; Zmwus1 partially suppresses Zmer1 enlarged-IM phenotype
Cytokinin signaling activates Type-B ARRs (AHK-AHP phosphorelay collapsed into this single gate)
Type-B ARRs directly bind AGATAT motifs in the ZmWUS1 proximal promoter and activate transcription
Type-B ARRs induce CK-responsive type-A response regulator ABPH1 (self-limiting feedback)
Type-A ARR ABPH1 provides negative feedback by damping CK -> ZmWUS1 activation (closes the canonical CK/WUS loop)
ZmWUS1 homeoprotein is transported via plasmodesmata to the apical domain to maintain the stem-cell pool
ZmIPT catalyzes rate-limiting CK biosynthesis (composite with LOG-family CK-activating enzymes)
ZmCKX cytokinin oxidase irreversibly degrades cytokinins (degradation arm of CK homeostasis)
UB3 directly binds LOG1 and Type-A ARR promoters, repressing CK biosynthesis and signaling (net CK down when UB3 up)
VT2 tryptophan aminotransferase (TAA1 co-ortholog) converts Trp to IPyA in IAA biosynthesis
SPI1 YUCCA-like flavin monooxygenase converts IPyA to IAA (local auxin biosynthesis)
Auxin is the TIR1/AFB F-box coreceptor ligand (Perception Gate; Auxin's only outgoing edge)
TIR1 SCF complex ubiquitinates BIF1 (and BIF4) Aux/IAA repressors for proteasomal degradation
Aux/IAA BIF1/BIF4 directly represses ARF-driven BA1 transcription until auxin-induced degradation removes them
ARF TFs bind AuxRE motifs in BA1 regulatory region and activate transcription when released from BIF1/BIF4
BIF2 PINOID-like kinase phosphorylates ZmPIN1a, regulating efflux carrier polarity in vivo
BIF2 kinase and BA1 co-localize in the nucleus; BIF2 functions by direct interaction with BA1 to promote AM initiation
Basipetal PAT by ZmPIN1a establishes auxin maxima at IM flanks that position axillary meristems; NPA inhibition abolishes AM formation
bHLH TF BA1 is required cell-autonomously for AM initiation; ba1 mutants completely lack axillary meristems
BA1 and BA2 cooperate as bHLH heterodimer partners; BA1 dosage affects ba2 phenotype indicating same-pathway action
BA2 bHLH protein heterodimerizes with BA1 and is required for AM initiation; ba2 fails to produce ears
ABPH1 positively regulates auxin levels and PIN1 signaling (CK-auxin crosstalk at IM periphery)
KN1 binds and upregulates tryptophan aminotransferase VT2 (auxin biosynthesis arm of the KNOX regulatory network)
KN1 ChIP-seq peaks near YUCCA-family auxin biosynthesis loci including SPI1, with up-regulation in Kn1-N
KN1 ChIP-seq peaks near PIN efflux transporter loci; Kn1-N GOF up-regulates PIN family (including ZmPIN1a)
KN1 positively regulates CK biosynthesis-related genes including the rice LONELY GUY ortholog (CK-activating enzyme, included in ZMIPT composite)
KN1 ChIP-seq peaks near AFB-like F-box auxin coreceptor genes (TIR1/AFB family)
KN1 class-I KNOX TF maintains indeterminate meristem identity; kn1 null produces a small cob with poor kernel yield
RA2 LOB-domain TF acts upstream of RA1 in ramosa pathway; ra1 expression is reduced in ra2 mutants
RA3 TPP enzyme acts upstream of RA1 via T6P/trehalose sugar-signaling input to SPM-determinacy program
FEA4 bZIP directly binds RA1 promoter and activates transcription (coherent FFL with RA2/RA3)
IDS1/Ts6 acts through multiple SPM-regulatory pathways including RA1 (coherent feed-forward with direct Ear_Branching effect)
RA1 binds the KN1 regulatory intron; RA1 and KN1 share ~189 overlapping target peaks
RA1 C2H2 zinc-finger TF imposes SPM determinacy and suppresses indeterminate ear branching
RA3 trehalose-6-phosphate phosphatase dephosphorylates T6P to trehalose, reducing the T6P sugar-signaling pool in SPMs
T6P sugar signal disrupts SPM determinacy and promotes indeterminate branching (inverse of the canonical SPM-determinacy axis); ra3 KO elevates T6P and yields branched ears
miR172 (encoded by ts4) directs cleavage of IDS1 AP2 mRNA
miR172 family members direct cleavage of SID1 AP2 mRNA
IDS1 AP2-domain TF represses indeterminate lateral branch meristem fates and promotes SPM determinacy (inverse of Ear_Branching)
SID1 AP2-domain TF (paralog of IDS1) redundantly represses indeterminate branching and promotes SPM determinacy
miR156 targets UB2 SBP-box mRNA for post-transcriptional silencing
miR156 targets UB3 SBP-box mRNA for post-transcriptional silencing
miR156 targets TSH4 SBP-box mRNA for post-transcriptional silencing
End-of-day far-red and WL+FR shade signals upregulate UB2 to drive the early SAM-to-IM transition (ub2;ub3 is insensitive to FR)
UB2 directly binds the UB3 promoter and the KRN4 distal enhancer via chromatin looping to positively regulate UB3 transcription (Du 2020; KRN4 QTL mechanism)
UB3 maintains KN1 expression in the central meristem; loss of KN1 in residual IM meristems in ub2;ub3
UB2/UB3/TSH4 directly bind BIF2 promoter and positively regulate transcription
UB2/UB3/TSH4 directly regulate ZmTCP30 promoter
UB3 restricts the rate of lateral-primordia initiation, limiting apparent IM expansion; ub3 KO yields enlarged IM and increased KRN
ZmTCP30 TCP-family TF supports normal ear development and grain number; Zmtcp30 mutants show reduced tassel branches and abnormal ears with reduced kernels
TSH4 SBP-box TF redundantly restricts SPM-initiation rate with UB2/UB3 (acts independently on KRN)
KRN2 WD40 protein interacts with DUF1644 in a complex that negatively regulates grain number; KRN2 CRISPR KO increased yield by ~10%
KRN5b 5-phosphatase mediates phosphoinositol signaling; higher-expression allele increases ear weight 10-12% via KRN
KNR6 Ser/Thr kinase phosphorylates AGAP in vitro; KNR6 OE increases kernels per row
AGAP ARF-GAP sustains IM proliferation; AGAP CRISPR KO produced shorter IMs by ~76 micrometer
Successful AM initiation is prerequisite to SPM formation and kernel row establishment
Larger IM produces more spikelet-pair meristems per tier, hence more kernel rows (E022 canonical mapping)
Indeterminate branching converts SPMs to branches and disrupts ordered rows, reducing kernel-row counts per ear
FEA4 bZIP TF directly upregulates BA1 transcription; BA1 is significantly downregulated in fea4 mutant ears
RA1 C2H2 zinc-finger TF imposes determinacy on spikelet-pair meristems, preventing indeterminate AM elaboration
UB2 SBP-box TF maintains KN1 expression in central meristem redundantly with UB3; loss of KN1 in ub2;ub3 residual IMs
UB2 SBP-box TF directly binds the BIF2 promoter; SBP-box consensus motif (GTAC) shared with UB3/TSH4 family
UB2 SBP-box TF directly binds and activates the ZmTCP30 promoter in parallel with UB3/TSH4
Cytokinin signalling induces ZmCKX cytokinin-oxidase transcription, closing the homeostatic negative-feedback loop (Motif 2-safe: CK->CKX->degrade CK is stabilising)
FEA4 bZIP directly activates RA3 TPP transcription; RA3 is significantly downregulated in fea4 mutants

FEA4 bZIP TF directly restricts kernel row number beyond its effects on IM size; Pautler et al. show fea4 mutant ears have increased kernel row number with enlarged IM and ectopic spikelet-pair meristem formation, implicating FEA4 in row-number restriction downstream of the meristem-size expansion phenotype.

REL2 TOPLESS-family corepressor is required for meristem restriction in maize ears; CRISPR rel2 single mutants show ear fasciation consistent with enlarged inflorescence meristem, independent of REL2's role in the RA-complex for branching control.
